skill.name: empathy-ledger-dev
skill.description: Project reference for building Empathy Ledger features with tenant isolation, OCAP principles, and cultural safety patterns.
skill.summary: Guides Empathy Ledger feature work with project structure, cultural protocols, and implementation patterns.
skill.author: Acurioustractor

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown code fences, inline identifiers, and reference names rather than executable behavior. No prompt injection attempt, network access, credential handling, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur in Markdown code fences and inline names. They describe project folders, sensitivity labels, services, slash commands, agents, and reference files, but do not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is visible Markdown documentation and contains no runtime wrapper, script block, or instruction to execute arbitrary shell input.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections point to prose and command names, including the skill description and design or API command labels. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, or encryption API appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural language and slash-command labels only, so the scanner likely matched short substrings rather than real cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections point to a TypeScript ownership comparison using user and story identifiers. The line does not call system information commands or collect host details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line compares application IDs for authorization logic and contains no operating system command or environment inspection behavior.

content.value_statement: Empathy Ledger feature work requires consistent tenant isolation, cultural safety, and data sovereignty patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a concise project reference for safer implementation choices.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes the Empathy Ledger project structure for Next.js, React components, services, AI code, and database types.
Documents OCAP principles and cultural sensitivity levels used by the platform.
Highlights tenant isolation expectations, including tenant_id usage and RLS boundaries.
Provides authentication and story ownership examples for API route work.
Lists key service areas, slash commands, specialized agents, and reference files.
limitations: Does not include the referenced CLAUDE.md, agent prompts, command files, or database type files.
Does not run tests, migrations, security scans, or project commands by itself.
Applies to the Empathy Ledger project and may not fit unrelated applications.
Does not replace legal, cultural, or security review by qualified project stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Story Feature
use_cases.description: Use the project structure and ownership rules before adding a story workflow or dashboard feature.

use_cases.title: Review Cultural Safety Requirements
use_cases.description: Check sensitivity levels, elder review expectations, and OCAP principles before changing story access behavior.

use_cases.title: Align Database and API Work
use_cases.description: Use the tenant isolation and authentication patterns when creating routes, migrations, and service changes.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Right Project Area
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Empathy Ledger development skill. I need to work on [feature]. Which app areas, components, services, and database domains should I inspect first?

prompt_templates.title: Check Cultural Sensitivity Impact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Empathy Ledger development skill. Review this proposed change for OCAP principles, cultural sensitivity levels, and elder approval impact: [describe change].

prompt_templates.title: Design an Authenticated API Route
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Empathy Ledger development skill. Plan an authenticated API route for [capability], including tenant isolation, story ownership checks, and likely service boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Cross-Domain Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Empathy Ledger development skill. Analyze this multi-domain change for tenant leakage, revocation behavior, consent impact, audit logging, and cultural protocol gaps: [change summary].

output_examples.input: Plan a new story sharing feature for trusted community members.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation checklist covering story ownership, sensitivity level handling, tenant boundaries, distribution tracking, revocation behavior, and elder review triggers.

output_examples.input: Review an API endpoint that returns story metadata.
output_examples.output: A security-focused review that checks authentication, tenant filtering, user ownership, cultural access limits, audit logging, and consent requirements.

output_examples.input: Choose where to add a new cultural approval workflow.
output_examples.output: A project navigation summary that points to cultural protocol types, service boundaries, relevant UI areas, and review commands to use next.

best_practices: Start every feature by identifying the tenant, story owner, consent state, and cultural sensitivity level.
Use existing service boundaries and database domains before adding new abstractions.
Request human review for sacred content, external sharing, revocation, and consent changes.
anti_patterns: Do not bypass tenant_id checks or assume organization context from client input alone.
Do not treat sacred or high sensitivity stories as normal shareable content.
Do not rely on this skill as the only source for legal, security, or cultural approval.

faq.question: What project is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for Empathy Ledger v2, a multi-tenant storytelling platform for Indigenous communities.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown reference and does not include executable scripts or command runners.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it verify OCAP compliance automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can guide review, but qualified community and project reviewers must make final decisions.

faq.question: Why was the security risk marked low?
faq.answer: Static detections were reviewed as false positives from Markdown formatting and inline identifiers.

faq.question: What files are included in the skill package?
faq.answer: The scanned package includes SKILL.md. Referenced project files are named but not included here.
